input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image that shows an engineering drawing of the vehicle on the top accompanied by six CAD views of the vehicle on the bottom. The six CAD views each feature a different orientation of our design, so that 3D information about our design can be inferred. In the CAD views, the relevant aerodynamic devices are highlighted in pink. The CAD views are provided to contextualize the engineering drawing, which has the same orientation as one of the six CAD views. All units displayed in the engineering drawing have units of mm. Based on the engineering drawing, does our design comply with rule T.7.5.b specified in the FSAE rule document? Only use dimensions explicitly shown in the engineering drawing to answer the question. If a dimension is not explicitly shown, you can assume that it complies with the rules. First provide an explanation for your answer (begin it with 'Explanation:'). Then provide just a yes/no answer (begin it with 'Answer:') that summarizes your response.

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.7.5
            TITLE: length

            CONTENT:
            rule T.7.5: Length
T.7.5 Length 
In plan view, any part of any Aerodynamic Device must be:  
a. No more than 700 mm forward of the fronts of the front tires 
b. No more than 250 mm rearward of the rear of the rear tires

intermediate_steps.founded_objects: dimension arrow, aerodynamic device, dimension line, tire outline, measurement number

final_answer:
Explanation:The rule T.7.5.b requires that any part of any Aerodynamic Device must be no more than 250 mm rearward of the rear of the rear tires. While the CAD views suggest that the aerodynamic device extends rearward of the rear tires, the engineering drawing does not explicitly provide a dimension quantifying the distance from the rear of the rear tires to the furthest rear point of the aerodynamic device. Following the instruction to only use explicitly shown dimensions, and assuming compliance when a dimension is not provided, the design is deemed compliant based on the available data.
Answer:Yes
